city.name: Bangalore
quickFacts: Major tech hub with MNCs and startups
Famous for Dosa, Bisi Bele Bath

alerts.title: Plastic Bag Ban in Karnataka
alerts.description: Single-use plastic bags are banned across Karnataka. Carry your own cloth or jute bag when shopping. Stores and vendors can be fined for providing plastic bags.

alerts.title: Helmet Mandatory for Two-Wheelers
alerts.description: Helmets are mandatory for both riders and pillion passengers on two-wheelers. Bangalore traffic police actively enforce this with spot fines of ₹500-₹1,000.

alerts.title: No Smoking in Public Places
alerts.description: Smoking is banned in restaurants, pubs, malls, parks, and public transport. Designated smoking zones exist in some establishments. Fines start at ₹200.

alerts.title: Liquor Shops Close by 10 PM
alerts.description: Retail liquor shops close at 10 PM. Bars and pubs in most areas have a deadline of 11:30 PM on weekdays and 1 AM on weekends. Plan your evenings accordingly.

alerts.title: FSSAI License for Food Vendors
alerts.description: All food establishments in Bangalore must display their FSSAI license. Look for the FSSAI logo when eating at new places — it ensures basic food safety and hygiene compliance.
alerts.source: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India

alerts.title: Masala Dosa — Bangalore's Iconic Breakfast
alerts.description: Masala Dosa is a must-try. Vidyarthi Bhavan (Gandhi Bazaar) and CTR (Malleshwaram) serve legendary versions. Eaten with coconut chutney and sambar. Best enjoyed fresh off the tawa — go early to avoid long queues.

alerts.title: Filter Coffee Culture
alerts.description: Bangalore runs on filter coffee (kaapi). Served in a steel tumbler and davara (cup and saucer set), it's made with freshly ground coffee and boiled milk. Indian Coffee House, Hatti Kaapi, and local darshinis are the best spots.

alerts.title: Bisi Bele Bath — Comfort Food of Karnataka
alerts.description: Bisi Bele Bath is a spicy rice-lentil dish unique to Karnataka. Served with boondi and chips at most darshinis (local quick-service restaurants). MTR on Lalbagh Road is famous for their version since 1924.

alerts.title: Darshini — Bangalore's Fast Food Culture
alerts.description: Darshinis are standing-only quick-service restaurants unique to Bangalore. Affordable South Indian meals — idli, vada, dosa, and meals (thali) served fast. No tipping expected. Just order at the counter, eat, and leave.

alerts.title: Kannada Language Basics
alerts.description: Learn a few Kannada words — 'Namaskara' (hello), 'Dhanyavada' (thank you), 'Howdu' (yes), 'Illa' (no). Locals genuinely appreciate the effort. Signboards and auto meters are often in Kannada script.

alerts.title: Idli-Vada — The Everyday Staple
alerts.description: Soft steamed idlis with crispy vada, served with sambar and multiple chutneys, is the everyday breakfast across Bangalore. Available at every darshini from 6 AM. Thatte Idli (plate-sized idli) from Bidadi on the Mysore highway is a local favourite.

alerts.title: Ragi Mudde — Traditional Karnataka Meal
alerts.description: Ragi Mudde (finger millet ball) eaten with spicy saaru (rasam) or bassaaru is a traditional Karnataka meal. Highly nutritious and filling. Try it at Naati Style or local Kannadiga restaurants for an authentic experience.

alerts.title: Temple Visit Etiquette
alerts.description: Bangalore has many temples like ISKCON, Bull Temple, and Dodda Ganapathi. Remove footwear before entering, dress modestly, and maintain silence inside. Accept prasadam (offering) with your right hand.

alerts.title: Bangalore Traffic — Plan Extra Time
alerts.description: Bangalore traffic is notoriously heavy, especially on Outer Ring Road, Silk Board junction, and Whitefield. Peak hours are 8:30-10:30 AM and 5:30-8:30 PM. Always add 30-45 min buffer. Use Google Maps for real-time routing.

alerts.title: Auto-Rickshaw Tips
alerts.description: Autos are everywhere but drivers often refuse short trips or quote high fares. Use Ola/Uber auto for metered rides. If hailing on the street, agree on the fare before getting in. Meter starts at ₹30.

alerts.title: Namma Metro — The Best Way Around
alerts.description: Namma Metro connects Majestic, MG Road, Indiranagar, Whitefield, and key areas. Buy a token or use a smart card. Runs from 5 AM to 11 PM. Fastest way to beat Bangalore traffic during peak hours.

alerts.title: VV Puram Food Street — Must Visit
alerts.description: VV Puram Food Street (Thindi Beedi) comes alive in the evenings with dozens of stalls. Try Congress Kadlekai (spiced peanuts), Dosa varieties, Holige (sweet flatbread), and Churmuri (puffed rice snack). Best visited between 6-9 PM.

alerts.title: Bangalore Weather — Always Pleasant
alerts.description: Bangalore enjoys mild weather year-round (15-30°C). Evenings can be cool — carry a light jacket. Monsoon runs June-September with evening showers. Rarely too hot, making it ideal for walking around and exploring.

alerts.title: Craft Beer Capital of India
alerts.description: Bangalore is India's craft beer hub with 50+ microbreweries. Toit, Arbor Brewing, and Windmills are popular picks. Most brewpubs are in Indiranagar, Koramangala, and Whitefield. Try a local Bangalore Bliss or Basmati Blonde.
